claims.text: On November 22, 1987, sportscaster Dan Roan's broadcast on WGN-TV Channel 9 in Chicago was interrupted by a pirate signal featuring a Max Headroom mask.

evidence.quote:
november 22nd 1987 was a pretty normal
evening for television viewers in
chicago
that night like every night dan roan was
covering sports on wgn tv channel 9.
then suddenly

claims.rationale: The Max Headroom broadcast-signal intrusion is documented by FCC records, multiple contemporaneous news reports, and surviving archival recordings. Date, station, and host all verified.

claims.text: Two hours after the WGN intrusion, the pirates hijacked PBS Channel 11 (WTTW) in the middle of a Doctor Who episode with a longer version of the Max Headroom segment.

evidence.quote:
ancock building but
the tv pirates weren't finished yet
two hours later on pbs channel 11 in the
middle of a doctor who episode
max was back and this time he had lots
to say
that is the only

claims.rationale: WTTW (Chicago PBS) had its signal hijacked during an episode of Doctor Who ('Horror of Fang Rock') on the same night — confirmed in WTTW's own statements and FCC records.

claims.text: The Max Headroom character originally appeared in the 1985 cyberpunk TV movie 'Max Headroom: 20 Minutes Into The Future' and ran as an ABC series from 1987 to 1988.

inally appeared in the
cyberpunk tv movie

in fact he had a show on the air at the
time it ran on channel 4 in t

claims.rationale: The 1985 Channel 4 TV movie and 1987-88 ABC series 'Max Headroom' are both well-documented in television history.

claims.text: Max Headroom was not really computer-generated — he was played by actor Matt Frewer in makeup and prosthetics sitting in front of a blue screen.

evidence.quote:

max was not really computerized he was
played by matt frewer who wore makeup
and prosthetics and sat in front of a
blue screen
max headroom was d

claims.rationale: Matt Frewer's prosthetics-based performance of Max Headroom is well-documented by the show's producers (Rocky Morton / Annabel Jankel) and Frewer's own interviews.

claims.text: The Max Headroom hacker's distorted voice was produced by running audio through an analog ring modulator — the same effect used for the Daleks on Doctor Who.

evidence.quote:
ow he created this voice
by running the audio feed through an
analog
ring modulator you can create this
effect and it might not have been an
accident that max hijack

claims.rationale: Audio analysis of the 1987 tape is consistent with ring-modulator processing, and the Dalek voice in Doctor Who is famously produced the same way — amateur technical analysis of the intrusion corroborates.

claims.text: A TV broadcast signal is sent on low power from the studio to a big antenna via a Studio Transmitter Link (STL), which the pirate exploited by overpowering the STL closer to the tower.

evidence.quote:
is the signal is
sent on low power from the studio to a
big antenna
this is called the studio transmitter
link or stl
then the big antenna takes the stl
amplifies it and br

claims.rationale: STL architecture is standard broadcast-engineering practice. The technique of overpowering an STL is the generally accepted explanation for the Max Headroom hack.

claims.text: The Captain Midnight HBO hijack on April 27, 1986 — during a broadcast of The Falcon and the Snowman — was America's first known broadcast-signal intrusion.

evidence.quote:
ion set
do not attempt to adjust the picture we
are controlling transmission april 27
1986 that night hbo broadcast the falcon
and the snowman
falcon in a winter soldier snowman yeah
i

claims.rationale: The Captain Midnight HBO hack on April 27, 1986 is well-documented and widely referenced as America's first major broadcast-signal intrusion.

claims.text: Captain Midnight was identified as John MacDougall, a satellite technician at a satellite uplink company in Ocala, Florida.

evidence.quote:
d the hacker
captain midnight was a satellite
technician named john mcdougall
who worked for a satellite uplink
company in ocala florida at the end of
his shift
mcdougall flipped a

claims.rationale: FCC records and contemporaneous reporting confirm John R. MacDougall of Ocala, Florida as the Captain Midnight hacker.

claims.text: MacDougall pleaded guilty to transmitting without a license, a violation of federal law, and paid a $5,000 fine.

evidence.quote:
arges of
transmitting without a license
a violation of federal law mcdougall
paid a five thousand dollar fine
now to this day mcdougall does

claims.rationale: MacDougall's plea and $5000 fine (under the Communications Act) are part of the public court record.

claims.text: To this day nobody has come forward as the Max Headroom hacker, and every speculative theory about the perpetrator's identity has eventually been debunked.

evidence.quote:
t there are tons of theories
eventually each theory is debunked
and to this day nobody has come forward
now most people believe that it was an
employee
with an axe to g

claims.rationale: The Max Headroom intrusion is famously unsolved as of the video's production. No arrest or identified perpetrator exists in the public record (despite recurring internet theories).

claims.text: The Max Headroom show was cancelled after being beaten in the ratings by Miami Vice.

evidence.quote:
the us from 1987 to 1988
the show was eventually cancelled when
it was beaten in the ratings by miami
vice
[Music]
oh it's tough to compete with crackit
and tubs it is
a

claims.rationale: ABC's Max Headroom was indeed cancelled after two short seasons; Friday-night competition from NBC's Miami Vice is often cited in retrospective explanations. 'Beaten by Miami Vice' is a popular but simplified explanation.

claims.text: The Max Headroom intrusion appeared to be done purely for curiosity and the glory of doing it — there was no message, no motive, and no perpetrator ever identified.

evidence.quote:
rind against wgn but
nobody really knows for sure
there was no message there was no motive
and decades later no perpetrator
it seems like it was done purely for the
curiosity
and t

claims.rationale: Host's explicit verdict framing: no message, no motive — done for the glory. This is an interpretive summary rather than a factual claim.
